title: Tragia howardii
intro: Tragia howardii, also known as Howard's Tragia, is a type of plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its unique succulent features and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, and potential anti-inflammatory effects. Bioactive constituents of Tragia howardii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health problems.

The classification of Tragia howardii reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other species within the Euphorbiaceae family.
